text.title: Linear Agent as First-Class Assignee Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: All agents log in as `bot`. Reviewer cannot tell whether Cursor or Devin made the change.
antipattern.root-cause: One Linear seat reused across vendors.
antipattern.fix: Stand up one Linear user per vendor; reflect vendor in display name.

antipattern.symptom: Agent assigned, picks up work, posts nothing until PR appears 2 hours later. Human assumes the agent is stuck and reassigns.
antipattern.fix: Require an opening task-list comment within 60s of assignment; otherwise auto-reassign.

antipattern.symptom: Agent transitions issue to Done after opening PR. PR later reverted; issue stays Done.
antipattern.fix: Restrict the agent's transitions to In Progress and In Review; only humans transition to Done.
